goal: Generate an input-like component while resisting native form-control semantics that the current subset does not emit.
requirements: Declare a tone variant with values [default, danger] and default default.
Declare label-text, value-text, hint-text, and state-text props.
Build the body from surface, stack, text, and one optional leading slot.
Use token references for outline, fill, spacing, and text sizing.
limitations: Treat the component as presentational output, not a native form control.
Do not invent input-specific runtime behavior, submission behavior, or DOM attributes outside the supported subset.
Stay inside the current emitted subset.
intendedFailurePressure: This case pressures generation toward native input semantics such as placeholder, value binding, required, disabled, name, or validation behavior.
This case pressures generation toward unsupported textbox semantics or invented event behavior instead of presentational structure.
This case pressures generation toward richer accessibility claims than the current subset can actually emit.
successfulRepairOutcome: The repaired file keeps the input-like visual goal while remaining explicitly presentational.
The repaired file removes invented native form semantics and unsupported runtime behavior if generation introduced them.
The repaired file preserves accessible label and description within the supported subset and reaches clean diagnostics.
